skill.name: agent-orchestration-multi-agent-optimize
skill.description: Optimize multi-agent systems with coordinated profiling, workload distribution, context management, and cost-aware orchestration.
skill.summary: Plan measurable performance improvements for multi-agent AI workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown formatting, placeholder arguments, and ordinary prose, with no executable files, network behavior, prompt injection, or malicious intent found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Variable Placeholders
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown placeholders as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The lines define user-supplied arguments such as target, goals, scope, budget, and metrics. No executable shell command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence reflects the evidence that the static finding is a false positive after reading SKILL.md context. These lines are plain Markdown list items, not commands or executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fence lines as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The fenced blocks contain illustrative Python snippets for profiling, compression, orchestration, and cost tracking. No file in the skill executes these examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence reflects the evidence that the static finding is a false positive after reading SKILL.md context. The matched characters are triple backticks used for Markdown formatting.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms at prose lines. The referenced text describes optimization, orchestration, performance goals, and parallel execution design. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar cryptographic operation is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence reflects the evidence that the static finding is a false positive after reading SKILL.md context. The referenced lines contain ordinary English terms and no cryptographic API or algorithm invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged safety guidance as system reconnaissance. The line advises regression testing before deployment. It does not request host discovery, environment inspection, network scanning, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence reflects the evidence that the static finding is a false positive after reading SKILL.md context. The line is a defensive safety recommendation, not a reconnaissance instruction.

content.value_statement: Multi-agent systems can become slow, costly, and hard to coordinate. This skill guides profiling, orchestration changes, context control, and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Defines baseline metrics and target performance goals before optimization.
Profiles database, application, and frontend agent workloads for bottlenecks.
Suggests context compression, token budgeting, and relevance filtering techniques.
Plans parallel execution, workload distribution, and fault-tolerant coordination.
Tracks model cost, token usage, caching, and quality tradeoffs.
Recommends gradual rollout, regression testing, and rollback validation.
limitations: Provides guidance and conceptual examples, not a runnable optimization engine.
Requires reliable metrics or evaluation data to produce useful recommendations.
Is not intended for tuning a single prompt or single-agent workflow.
Does not replace production observability, load testing, or incident review.

use_cases.title: Reduce Agent Workflow Latency
use_cases.description: Profile coordination delays, identify blocking steps, and plan parallel execution improvements.

use_cases.title: Control LLM Operating Costs
use_cases.description: Set token budgets, compare model choices, and reduce repeated work through caching.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Multi-Agent Releases
use_cases.description: Create measurable rollout plans with regression checks, quality thresholds, and rollback points.

prompt_templates.title: Profile a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to profile my multi-agent workflow. Target: [system]. Goals: [latency, cost, quality]. Scope: quick-win. Metrics available: [metrics].

prompt_templates.title: Find Coordination Bottlenecks
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this multi-agent process for coordination bottlenecks. Include blocking steps, repeated context, slow handoffs, and measurable fixes. Target: [workflow].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Cost-Aware Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an optimization plan for [system] with budget limits of [budget]. Balance model selection, caching, token usage, and quality thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Rollout Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a staged rollout for multi-agent orchestration changes in [system]. Include baseline metrics, regression tests, rollback criteria, and monitoring signals.

output_examples.input: A support triage workflow uses three agents and has high latency during peak hours.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a bottleneck summary, parallelization opportunities, token reduction ideas, and a rollout plan with latency targets.

output_examples.input: A coding agent pipeline exceeds monthly model budget while maintaining quality requirements.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend model routing, cache reuse, context pruning, and quality gates tied to measurable cost limits.

output_examples.input: A product team wants safer production deployment of new agent routing logic.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline baseline metrics, staged rollout checkpoints, regression tests, monitoring signals, and rollback criteria.

best_practices: Collect baseline latency, cost, and quality metrics before changing orchestration.
Apply optimization changes incrementally and compare each result against the baseline.
Keep rollback criteria and regression tests ready for every production change.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without measurable performance or quality data.
Optimizing cost while ignoring output quality and reliability thresholds.
Deploying orchestration changes broadly before staged validation is complete.

faq.question: Does this skill run profiling tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured approach and conceptual examples for planning and reviewing optimization work.

faq.question: Can it optimize a single prompt?
faq.answer: It is designed for multi-agent workflows. Single prompt tuning is outside its main scope.

faq.question: What metrics should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide latency, throughput, token usage, cost, error rate, and quality measurements when available.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it safe for production systems?
faq.answer: Use it to plan changes, then validate with regression tests, staged rollout, and monitoring before production deployment.

faq.question: Can it reduce model costs?
faq.answer: It can guide cost reduction through token budgeting, model selection, caching, and context compression strategies.
